prompt:
Write a function `number_to_words(n)` that converts a non-negative integer (0 to 999) into its English words representation, as you might see printed on a check.

Rules:
- Use lowercase words separated by single spaces.
- No hyphens or commas.
- If n is 0, return "zero".

Examples:
  number_to_words(0)   → "zero"
  number_to_words(5)   → "five"
  number_to_words(13)  → "thirteen"
  number_to_words(42)  → "forty two"
  number_to_words(100) → "one hundred"
  number_to_words(115) → "one hundred fifteen"
  number_to_words(999) → "nine hundred ninety nine"

Constraints:
- 0 <= n <= 999
- n is always an integer
validation_trace: Traced reference solution against all 15 test cases: zero→'zero', 1→'one', 5→'five', 10→'ten', 11→'eleven', 13→'thirteen', 19→'nineteen', 20→'twenty', 42→'forty two', 90→'ninety', 100→'one hundred', 115→'one hundred fifteen', 300→'three hundred', 501→'five hundred one', 999→'nine hundred ninety nine'. All outputs match expected values.
